channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: JDK 25: The new features in Java 25
item.description: Java Development Kit (JDK) 25, a planned long-term support release of standard Java due in September, now has two features officially proposed for it. The first is a preview of an API for stable values, a feature that promises to speed up the startup of Java applications.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Vance hopeful 'high-level' TikTok deal will be completed by early April
item.description: Vice President JD Vance said Friday that he was hopeful a deal to keep TikTok operating in the U.S. will be wrapped up by the early April deadline.
item.dc:creator: Haleluya Hadero, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: For March’s Patch Tuesday, 57 fixes
item.description: For so few patches from Microsoft this month (57), we have seven zero-days to manage (with a “Patch Now” recommendation for Windows) and standard release schedules for Microsoft Office, Microsoft browsers (Edge) and Visual Studio.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Details of Nvidia's fastest video card ever leak; RTX Pro 6000 Blackwell GPU will have 96GB GDDR7 ECC memory
item.description: Details of Nvidia's fastest video card ever, the RTX Pro 6000 Blackwell GPU, have been spotted online.
item.dc:creator: waynewilliams@onmail.com (Wayne Williams), TechRadar

item.title: You can now use an IPv4 address as business collateral - and it could be worth millions
item.description: You can now secure a loan using your IPv4 address as collateral – you’ll even still be able to access and use it.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: West Virginia football coach Rich Rodriguez isn’t here for locker room dances
item.description: From the White House to the local police department, TikTok trends are now part of the cultural conversation—for better or worse. But don’t expect to see the latest dance craze in the West Virginia football team’s locker room.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: How technology can lower barriers to employment for Californians with disabilities
item.description: New technology — and its rapid adoption for remote work during COVID-19 — continues to break down many barriers for people with disabilities.
item.dc:creator: CalMatters, The Mercury News

item.title: Tesla says it is 'exposed' to retaliatory tariffs
item.description: Tesla warned U.S. Trade Representative Jamieson Greer that the electric vehicle (EV) company and other American exporters are “inherently exposed” to retaliatory tariffs levied in response to ones from the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Apple plans AirPods feature that can live-translate conversations
item.description: (Bloomberg) -- Apple Inc. is planning a new AirPods feature that allows the earbuds to live-translate an in-person conversation into another language, according to people with knowledge of the matter.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Silent Hill f will present players with 'a beautiful yet terrifying choice', and I can't wait to see what it is
item.description: Silent Hill f is the series' first game to be set in Japan, and its blend of beauty and terror looks absolutely horrifying.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Windows 10 Insider Previews: A guide to the builds
item.description: Microsoft never sleeps. In addition to its steady releases of major and minor updates to the current version of Windows 10, the company frequently rolls out public preview builds to members of its Windows Insider Program, allowing them to test out — and even help shape — upcoming features.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: IT leaders are driving a new cloud computing era
item.description: At fictional Tech Innovations Inc., a midsize software company, the rapid adoption of cloud technology was once seen as a transformative stepping stone toward agility and scalability.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: StudentAid.gov website went down, following mass layoffs at Department of Education
item.description: An hourslong outage Wednesday on StudentAid.gov, the federal website for student loans and financial aid, underscored the risks in rapidly gutting the Department of Education, as President Donald Trump aims to dismantle the agency.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Arbitrator sides with Meta in bid to halt sales of ex-employee's memoir
item.description: An arbitrator has sided with Meta in its case against a former employee who made a series of misconduct allegations about the Facebook and Instagram parent company in a memoir published this week.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Greece says ambitious Mediterranean power cable project is on track despite setbacks
item.description: ATHENS, Greece (AP) — The future of an ambitious electricity cable linking the power grids of Greece, Cyprus and Israel by the end of the decade is on track despite a series of setbacks, Greece’s Foreign Minister Giorgos Gerapetritis said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: OpenAI calls for US to centralize AI regulation
item.description: OpenAI executives think the federal government should regulate artificial intelligence in the US, taking precedence over often more restrictive state regulations.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Cisco’s chief product officer explains the company’s transformation around AI
item.description: For 40 years, Cisco has been best known for building routers, switches, and other networking technology that connects computers within offices, data centers, and across the internet.
item.dc:creator: Steven Melendez, Fast Company

item.title: SpaceX delays flight to retrieve astronauts
item.description: NASA and SpaceX canceled Wednesday’s planned launch of the Crew-10 mission to retrieve astronauts from the International Space Station (ISS), citing an issue with the launch pad.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Meta to start testing crowd-sourced fact-checking, based on X example, next week
item.description: Facebook and Instagram parent company Meta Platforms Inc. said Thursday it will begin testing its crowd-sourced fact-checking program, Community Notes, on March 18. It will initially based on a ratings system used by Elon Musk's X. Meta ended its fact-checking program in January.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Tech workers in Kenya hold vigil for TikTok content moderator who died
item.description: Technology workers in Kenya have held a vigil for a colleague who died in unclear circumstances after she was unable to travel to her home in Nigeria for two years.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Gov tech layoffs could hamper SHARE IT Act software mandates
item.description: The SHARE IT Act requires agencies to share custom-developed software code. But the termination of 18F and other tech experts could hamper the law's goals. The post Gov tech layoffs could hamper SHARE IT Act software mandates first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: OpenAI takes on rivals with new Responses API, Agents SDK
item.description: OpenAI’s new Responses API and an upgraded Agents SDK will help enterprises more easily build agents with advanced reasoning and multimodal capabilities, the company said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Trump says TikTok deal is in the works. Here's where things stand with the company
item.description: In less than a month, TikTok could have one or a few new owners, be banned again, or simply receive another reprieve to continue operating in the United States.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Dangerous malware targets you through fake loan app
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says a malware app called SpyLend is posing as a financial tool, hitting 100,000-plus downloads.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Computer owners duped in antivirus scam to get more than $25M
item.description: More than $25 million in payments will go out this week to customers who were tricked into paying for fraudulent tech support offered by two companies.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, NewsNation

item.title: 3 of the best LLM integration tools for R
item.description: When we first looked at this space in late 2023, many generative AI R packages focused largely on OpenAI and ChatGPT-like functionality or coding add-ins. Today, the landscape includes packages that natively support more LLM suppliers—including models run locally on your own computer.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Elon Musk claims X being targeted in 'massive cyberattack' as service goes down
item.description: Hours after a series of outages Monday that left X unavailable to thousands of users, Elon Musk claimed that the social media platform was being targeted in a “massive cyberattack.” “We get attacked every day, but this was done with a lot of resources," Musk claimed in a post.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE CHAPMAN and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Fortran, Delphi rise in Tiobe popularity index
item.description: Older programming languages such as Fortran and Delphi are making a comeback, based on the results of this month’s Tiobe index of language popularity. Tiobe attributes their rise to the need to maintain vital legacy systems still dependent on them.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Got a bank transfer alert text? It might be a scam; here’s what to do
item.description: Kurt the CyberGuy offers tips to safeguard your personal information as scammers become more sophisticated with scam texts and other methods.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: Helping DOGE cut waste
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Chinese humanoid robot lands world's first front flip
item.description: A robotics firm in China claims a robot has performed the world's first humanoid robot front flip, which is significantly more difficult than a backflip.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
